episodes.llm_raw:
```json
{
  "schema_version": "1.0",
  "project_id": "Reduce_Packaging_Defects_001",
  "project_name": "Reduce Packaging Defects",
  "industry": "Manufacturing",
  "process_area": "Production",
  "pain_theme": "Quality",
  "problem_statement": "Defect pada inner packaging meningkat dari 2.5% menjadi 6% dalam 3 bulan terakhir, terutama pada sealing line. Hal ini menyebabkan rework dan scrap meningkat signifikan.",
  "goal_statement": "Reduce defect",
  "business_case": "Meningkatkan kualitas produk dan mengurangi biaya rework serta scrap akan meningkatkan kepuasan pelanggan dan profitabilitas perusahaan.",
  "project_scope": {
    "in_scope": [
      "Sealing line operations",
      "Inner packaging processes",
      "Training and quality control measures"
    ],
    "out_of_scope": [
      "Outer packaging processes",
      "Non-production related issues",
      "Supplier management"
    ]
  },
  "ctq_list": [
    {
      "name": "Inner Packaging Defect Rate",
      "description": "The percentage of inner packaging that fails to meet quality standards due to defects.",
      "metric": "Defect Rate",
      "unit": "%"
    },
    {
      "name": "Rework Rate on Sealing Line",
      "description": "The percentage of products that require rework due to defects in the sealing process.",
      "metric": "Rework Rate",
      "unit": "%"
    },
    {
      "name": "Scrap Cost",
      "description": "The total cost incurred due to scrap resulting from defective inner packaging.",
      "metric": "Total Cost",
      "unit": "Currency"
    }
  ],
  "y_variable": "Inner Packaging Defect Rate",
  "x_categories": {
    "Man": [
      "Operator training on sealing process",
      "Staffing levels on sealing line"
    ],
    "Machine": [
      "Condition of sealing jaws",
      "Calibration of sealing equipment",
      "Heat stability of sealing jaws"
    ],
    "Method": [
      "Sealing process standard operating procedures",
      "Quality control checks during sealing"
    ],
    "Material": [
      "Quality of packaging materials",
      "Supplier consistency"
    ],
    "Measurement": [
      "Defect tracking system",
      "Inspection frequency"
    ],
    "Environment": [
      "Temperature and humidity in production area",
      "Maintenance schedules"
    ]
  },
  "sipoc": {
    "Suppliers": ["Packaging material suppliers", "Equipment manufacturers"],
    "Inputs": ["Packaging materials", "Sealing equipment", "Operator training"],
    "Process": ["Sealing process", "Quality control checks"],
    "Outputs": ["Inner packaged products"],
    "Customers": ["End customers", "Retailers"]
  },
  "early_measure_plan": {
    "measures": [
      "Monitor defect rates weekly",
      "Track rework and scrap costs bi-weekly",
      "Conduct regular inspections of sealing equipment"
    ],
    "responsible": "Quality Assurance Team"
  },
  "risk_level": "Medium",
  "project_type": "Quality Improvement"
}
```

episodes.goal_statement: Reduce the inner packaging defect rate from 6% to 2% within the next 6 months.

ctq_list.name: Inner Packaging Defect Rate
ctq_list.description: The percentage of inner packaging that fails to meet quality standards due to defects, aiming for a target of 2%.

ctq_list.name: Rework Rate on Sealing Line
ctq_list.description: The percentage of products that require rework due to defects in the sealing process, aiming for a reduction to 5%.

ctq_list.name: Scrap Cost
ctq_list.description: The total cost incurred due to scrap resulting from defective inner packaging, aiming to reduce costs by 30%.

measures: Monitor defect rates weekly with detailed reporting
Track rework and scrap costs bi-weekly with variance analysis
Conduct regular inspections of sealing equipment with documented findings and action items
early_measure_plan.responsible: Quality Assurance Team, with weekly updates to project stakeholders

episodes.goal_statement: Reduce moisture content from 16% to 15% within the next 3 months, ensuring compliance with specification limits.

in_scope: Quality control measures related to moisture content
Training for operators on moisture management with a defined curriculum and schedule
out_of_scope: Other quality metrics not related to moisture content
Supplier management outside of moisture-related issues

ctq_list.name: Moisture Content
ctq_list.description: The amount of moisture present in the product, aiming for a maximum of 15%.

ctq_list.name: Product Consistency
ctq_list.description: The uniformity of moisture content across different batches.

ctq_list.name: Product Quality Rating
ctq_list.description: Overall quality assessment based on moisture content and other factors, defined by specific criteria.

measures: Monitor moisture content weekly with baseline measurements established
Track product quality ratings bi-weekly with defined criteria
Conduct regular training sessions for operators based on a detailed curriculum

episodes.risk_level: Medium with a detailed risk assessment and mitigation plan to be developed.

episodes.problem_statement: Throughput proses filter press saat ini 5 ton/jam sedangkan kapasitas terpasang adalah 8 ton per jam. Penyebab potensial termasuk malfungsi peralatan dan ketidakefisienan operator.
episodes.goal_statement: Meningkatkan throughput menjadi mendekati kapasitas terpasang 8 ton/jam dalam waktu 6 bulan.
episodes.business_case: Meningkatkan throughput akan mengurangi biaya per ton dan meningkatkan profitabilitas perusahaan, dengan estimasi penghematan biaya sebesar 15% per ton.

ctq_list.name: Throughput Rate
ctq_list.description: Jumlah material yang diproses oleh filter press per jam.

ctq_list.name: Cost per Ton
ctq_list.description: Total biaya yang dikeluarkan untuk memproses satu ton material melalui filter press.

ctq_list.name: Operational Efficiency
ctq_list.description: Rasio antara throughput aktual dengan kapasitas maksimum filter press.

ctq_list.name: Product Quality
ctq_list.description: Kualitas produk akhir yang dihasilkan dari proses filter press.

Man: Pelatihan operator pada operasi filter press
Tingkat staf selama waktu produksi puncak
Machine: Kondisi dan pemeliharaan peralatan filter press
Method: Prosedur operasi standar untuk operasi filter press
Langkah-langkah pengendalian proses untuk memastikan kinerja optimal
Material: Kualitas material input yang diproses
Konsistensi karakteristik material yang mempengaruhi throughput
Environment: Kontrol suhu dan kelembapan di area produksi

measures: Monitor throughput rates setiap minggu dengan target 6 ton/jam dalam 3 bulan
Lacak biaya per ton setiap dua minggu dengan target pengurangan 10%
Lakukan inspeksi rutin pada peralatan filter press dan catat hasilnya

episodes.risk_level: Medium - Risiko terkait malfungsi peralatan dan ketidakpuasan operator dapat mempengaruhi hasil proyek.

episodes.summary_md:
## DEFINE PHASE SUMMARY — Increase Throughput Line 1

### Problem Statement
Throughput line 1 was currently 5 tons/hour from January to August 2025, while the target is 7 tons/hour with an installed capacity of 10 tons/hour. This creates a higher cost per ton of product due to lower output.

### Goal Statement
Increase throughput from 5 tons/hour to 7 tons/hour.

### Business Case
Achieving the target throughput will reduce the cost per ton of product, enhancing profitability and operational efficiency.

### Scope
- In Scope: Throughput line operations, Training programs for operators, Monitoring and maintenance of production equipment
- Out of Scope: Other production lines not related to throughput line 1, Non-production related issues, Supplier management outside of material quality

### CTQs
- Throughput Rate: The amount of product produced per hour on the throughput line, aiming for a target of 7 tons/hour.
- Cost per Ton: The total cost incurred to produce one ton of product, aiming to reduce costs through increased throughput.
- Installed Capacity Utilization: The percentage of the installed capacity that is being utilized for production, aiming for optimal utilization.

episodes.business_case: Achieving the target throughput will reduce the cost per ton of product, enhancing profitability and operational efficiency.

episodes.summary_md:
## DEFINE PHASE SUMMARY — proj_003_ Late_delivery

### Problem Statement
It's been more than 3 times that shipments to customer AAJ in Jakarta are consistently late, averaging 5-6 days beyond the scheduled delivery date.

### Goal Statement
Achieve 100% on-time delivery with no late shipments.

### Business Case
Improving delivery timeliness will enhance customer satisfaction, reduce complaints, and potentially increase repeat business, leading to higher profitability.

### Scope
- In Scope: Supply chain operations, Logistics and delivery processes, Scheduling and tracking improvements, Training for staff involved in delivery, Production processes related to delivery schedules
- Out of Scope: Supplier management outside of delivery impact, Non-logistics related customer service issues

### CTQs
- **On-Time Delivery Rate**(Number of on-time deliveries %) — The percentage of shipments delivered to the customer on or before the scheduled delivery date.
- **Average Delay Duration**(Average delay in days days) — The average number of days that shipments are delayed beyond the scheduled delivery date.
- **Customer Satisfaction Score**(Customer satisfaction rating scale (1-10)) — A measure of customer satisfaction regarding the timeliness of delivery.
episodes.problem_text: Shipments to customer AAJ in Jakarta have been consistently late, averaging 5-6 days beyond the scheduled delivery date, occurring more than three times.
episodes.goal_statement: Achieve 100% on-time delivery with no late shipments by the end of Q2 2026.
episodes.business_case: Improving delivery timeliness will enhance customer satisfaction, reduce complaints, and potentially increase repeat business, leading to an estimated 15% increase in profitability.

in_scope: Training for staff involved in delivery
Production processes related to delivery schedules
out_of_scope: Supplier management outside of delivery impact, specifically contract negotiations and supplier selection
Non-logistics related customer service issues such as product quality complaints

ctq_list.name: On-Time Delivery Rate
ctq_list.description: The percentage of shipments delivered to the customer on or before the scheduled delivery date.

ctq_list.name: Average Delay Duration
ctq_list.description: The average number of days that shipments are delayed beyond the scheduled delivery date.

ctq_list.name: Customer Satisfaction Score
ctq_list.description: A measure of customer satisfaction regarding the timeliness of delivery.

measures: Monitor on-time delivery rates weekly with a target of 85% by the end of Q1 2026
Track average delay durations bi-weekly aiming to reduce to 3 days by Q2 2026
Conduct customer satisfaction surveys monthly with a goal to increase the score to 8 by Q2 2026

episodes.risk_level: Medium - Risks include potential disruptions from traffic conditions and weather-related issues in Jakarta, as well as internal process inefficiencies.

episodes.summary_md:
## DEFINE PHASE SUMMARY — Increase Throughput Line 1

### Problem Statement
Throughput line 1 was currently 5 ton/hour from January to August 2025, while the target is 7 ton/hour with an installed capacity of 10 ton/hour. This creates a higher cost per ton of product due to lower output.

### Goal Statement
Increase throughput from 5 ton/hour to 7 ton/hour.

### Business Case
Increasing throughput will lower the cost per ton of product, improving overall cost efficiency and profitability.

### Scope
- In Scope: Line 1 production operations, Operator training programs, Maintenance and calibration of production equipment, Quality control measures during production
- Out of Scope: Other production lines, Non-production related issues, Supplier management outside of raw material quality

### CTQs
- **Throughput Rate**(Tons per hour ton/h) — The rate at which the production line processes material, aiming for a target of 7 ton/hour.
- **Cost per Ton**(Cost currency/ton) — The total cost incurred to produce one ton of product, aiming to reduce this cost through increased throughput.
- **Installed Capacity Utilization**(Utilization Rate %) — The percentage of the installed capacity that is being used for production, aiming for optimal utilization.

inputs.problem_text: its been more than 3 times, shipment to customer aaj in jakarta always late than schedule, in average 5-6 days late.

outputs.problem_statement: Shipments to customers in Jakarta have been consistently late, averaging 5-6 days beyond the scheduled delivery date, affecting customer satisfaction.
outputs.goal_statement: Achieve 100% on-time delivery for all shipments within the next 6 months.
outputs.business_case: Improving on-time delivery will enhance customer satisfaction, reduce complaints, and strengthen the company's reputation in the market, ultimately leading to increased sales and customer loyalty.

in_scope: Logistics operations related to shipment processing
Communication with customers regarding delivery timelines
out_of_scope: Supplier management and procurement processes
Production scheduling unrelated to delivery

ctq_list.name: On-Time Delivery Rate
ctq_list.description: The percentage of shipments delivered on or before the scheduled delivery date.

ctq_list.name: Average Delay Duration
ctq_list.description: The average number of days shipments are delayed beyond the scheduled delivery date.

ctq_list.name: Customer Satisfaction Score
ctq_list.description: The level of customer satisfaction regarding delivery timeliness, measured through surveys.

Man: Training for logistics personnel on delivery schedules
Staffing levels in logistics and transportation
Method: Standard operating procedures for shipment processing
Material: Quality of packaging materials affecting delivery speed
Availability of inventory for timely shipments

measures: Monitor on-time delivery rates weekly
Track average delay duration bi-weekly
Conduct customer satisfaction surveys quarterly
early_measure_plan.data_source: Logistics tracking system and customer feedback surveys

episodes.insight_md:
### Agent Insight for proj_003_Late_delivery

- **Justification of CTQ/Y/Scope**: The primary CTQ, "On-Time Delivery Rate," directly addresses the critical customer expectation of timely shipments, which is crucial for customer satisfaction and retention. The scope focuses on logistics operations and communication, ensuring that all factors influencing delivery are considered.

- **Key Risks/Assumptions**: 
  - Assumes that training logistics personnel will significantly improve delivery timelines.
  - Risks include potential disruptions from external factors like traffic and weather, which may not be controllable.
  - Dependence on the efficiency of routing software and vehicle conditions could impact delivery performance.

- **Learning from Past Episodes**: Similar projects have successfully improved delivery performance by enhancing training programs and standard operating procedures. Past audits indicate that effective communication with customers regarding delays significantly boosts satisfaction.

- **Actionable Next Steps for Measure**:
  1. Establish a baseline by monitoring the current on-time delivery rates and average delay durations over the next month.
  2. Implement a tracking system for real-time shipment status updates to identify bottlenecks in the delivery process.
  3. Conduct a survey to gauge customer satisfaction regarding delivery timelines and communication, to inform improvement strategies.

inputs.problem_text:
A/R is part of cashflow performance. In 2016,  36.20% of total A/R overdue in ID03 was trigger by PT Smart, so we will focus on this customer for this project.
High A/R overdue shows that a company is selling its product to customer on credit and taking longer to collect money. It means company has less readily available cash to finance our operation. In addition, high A/R overdue will be blocked the orders and CSD cannot send the product until the payment has been made by PT Smart.
inputs.goal_text: Reduce  monthly overdue A/R PT Smart to 12.8 %  by end of Nov 2017.

episodes.insight_md:
- **Justification for CTQ/Y/Scope:**
  - The primary CTQ, "A/R Overdue Percentage," directly impacts cash flow and operational efficiency, making it crucial for project focus.
  - Focusing on PT Smart, which accounts for 36.20% of overdue A/R, aligns with the goal to improve cash flow performance significantly.
  - The project scope includes critical areas like accounts receivable processes and credit policy reviews, ensuring comprehensive coverage of factors affecting A/R overdue.

- **Key Risks/Assumptions:**
  - Assumes that changes in credit policies and collection strategies will significantly reduce overdue A/R.
  - Economic conditions could impact customer payment behavior, posing a risk to achieving the desired A/R overdue reduction.
  - Assumes that the finance team will effectively implement new training and strategies to improve collection rates.

- **Learning from Similar Past Episodes/Audit:**
  - Previous projects highlighted the importance of targeted training for the finance team in reducing A/R overdue percentages.
  - Past audits indicated that automated reminders and improved credit evaluations can significantly impact overdue reduction.

- **Actionable Next Steps for Measure:**
  - Collect and analyze historical data on A/R overdue percentages and DSO for PT Smart to establish a baseline.
  - Implement tracking mechanisms for blocked orders due to overdue A/R to identify patterns and root causes.
  - Engage with the finance team to review and refine data collection methods, ensuring accuracy and consistency in measurement.

A/R is part of cashflow performance. In 2016,  36.20% of total A/R overdue in ID03 was trigger by PT Smart, so we will focus on this customer for this project.

similar_memory_episodes.summary_md:
## DEFINE PHASE SUMMARY — Increase Throughput Line 1

### Problem Statement
Throughput line 1 was currently 5 tons/hour from January to August 2025, while the target is 7 tons/hour with an installed capacity of 10 tons/hour. This creates a higher cost per ton of product due to lower output.

### Goal Statement
Increase throughput from 5 tons/hour to 7 tons/hour.

### Business Case
Achieving the target throughput will reduce the cost per ton of product, enhancing profitability and operational efficiency.

### Scope
- In Scope: Throughput line operations, Training programs for operators, Monitoring and maintenance of production equipment
- Out of Scope: Other production lines not related to throughput line 1, Non-production related issues, Supplier management outside of material quality

### CTQs
- Throughput Rate: The amount of product produced per hour on the throughput line, aiming for a target of 7 tons/hour.
- Cost per Ton: The total cost incurred to produce one ton of product, aiming to reduce costs through increased throughput.
- Installed Capacity Utilization: The percentage of the installed capacity that is being utilized for production, aiming for optimal utilization.

similar_memory_episodes.summary_md:
## DEFINE PHASE SUMMARY — Increase Throughput Line 1

### Problem Statement
Throughput line 1 was currently 5 ton/hour from January to August 2025, while the target is 7 ton/hour with an installed capacity of 10 ton/hour. This creates a higher cost per ton of product due to lower output.

### Goal Statement
Increase throughput from 5 ton/hour to 7 ton/hour.

### Business Case
Increasing throughput will lower the cost per ton of product, improving overall cost efficiency and profitability.

### Scope
- In Scope: Line 1 production operations, Operator training programs, Maintenance and calibration of production equipment, Quality control measures during production
- Out of Scope: Other production lines, Non-production related issues, Supplier management outside of raw material quality

### CTQs
- **Throughput Rate**(Tons per hour ton/h) — The rate at which the production line processes material, aiming for a target of 7 ton/hour.
- **Cost per Ton**(Cost currency/ton) — The total cost incurred to produce one ton of product, aiming to reduce this cost through increased throughput.
- **Installed Capacity Utilization**(Utilization Rate %) — The percentage of the installed capacity that is being used for production, aiming for optimal utilization.

inputs.problem_text: its been more than 3 times, shipment to customer aaj in jakarta always late than schedule, in average 5-6 days late.

outputs.problem_statement: It's been more than 3 times that shipments to customer AAJ in Jakarta are consistently late, averaging 5-6 days beyond the scheduled delivery date.
outputs.goal_statement: Achieve 100% on-time delivery with no late shipments.
outputs.business_case: Improving delivery timeliness will enhance customer satisfaction, reduce complaints, and potentially increase repeat business, leading to higher profitability.

in_scope: Training for staff involved in delivery
Production processes related to delivery schedules
out_of_scope: Supplier management outside of delivery impact
Non-logistics related customer service issues

ctq_list.name: On-Time Delivery Rate
ctq_list.description: The percentage of shipments delivered to the customer on or before the scheduled delivery date.

ctq_list.name: Average Delay Duration
ctq_list.description: The average number of days that shipments are delayed beyond the scheduled delivery date.

ctq_list.name: Customer Satisfaction Score
ctq_list.description: A measure of customer satisfaction regarding the timeliness of delivery.

Man: Training for logistics and delivery personnel
Staffing levels in the supply chain department
Method: Standard operating procedures for order processing
Material: Quality and reliability of packaging materials
Availability of inventory for timely shipments
Measurement: Tracking of delivery times and delays

measures: Monitor on-time delivery rates weekly
Track average delay durations bi-weekly
Conduct customer satisfaction surveys monthly

similar_memory_episodes.summary_md:
## DEFINE PHASE SUMMARY — proj_003_ Late_delivery

### Problem Statement
It's been more than 3 times that shipments to customer AAJ in Jakarta are consistently late, averaging 5-6 days beyond the scheduled delivery date.

### Goal Statement
Achieve 100% on-time delivery with no late shipments.

### Business Case
Improving delivery timeliness will enhance customer satisfaction, reduce complaints, and potentially increase repeat business, leading to higher profitability.

### Scope
- In Scope: Supply chain operations, Logistics and delivery processes, Scheduling and tracking improvements, Training for staff involved in delivery, Production processes related to delivery schedules
- Out of Scope: Supplier management outside of delivery impact, Non-logistics related customer service issues

### CTQs
- **On-Time Delivery Rate**(Number of on-time deliveries %) — The percentage of shipments delivered to the customer on or before the scheduled delivery date.
- **Average Delay Duration**(Average delay in days days) — The average number of days that shipments are delayed beyond the scheduled delivery date.
- **Customer Satisfaction Score**(Customer satisfaction rating scale (1-10)) — A measure of customer satisfaction regarding the timeliness of delivery.
similar_memory_episodes.problem_text: Shipments to customer AAJ in Jakarta have been consistently late, averaging 5-6 days beyond the scheduled delivery date, occurring more than three times.
similar_memory_episodes.goal_statement: Achieve 100% on-time delivery with no late shipments by the end of Q2 2026.
similar_memory_episodes.business_case: Improving delivery timeliness will enhance customer satisfaction, reduce complaints, and potentially increase repeat business, leading to an estimated 15% increase in profitability.

in_scope: Training for staff involved in delivery
Production processes related to delivery schedules
out_of_scope: Supplier management outside of delivery impact, specifically contract negotiations and supplier selection
Non-logistics related customer service issues such as product quality complaints

ctq_list.name: On-Time Delivery Rate
ctq_list.description: The percentage of shipments delivered to the customer on or before the scheduled delivery date.

ctq_list.name: Average Delay Duration
ctq_list.description: The average number of days that shipments are delayed beyond the scheduled delivery date.

ctq_list.name: Customer Satisfaction Score
ctq_list.description: A measure of customer satisfaction regarding the timeliness of delivery.

measures: Monitor on-time delivery rates weekly with a target of 85% by the end of Q1 2026
Track average delay durations bi-weekly aiming to reduce to 3 days by Q2 2026
Conduct customer satisfaction surveys monthly with a goal to increase the score to 8 by Q2 2026

similar_memory_episodes.risk_level: Medium - Risks include potential disruptions from traffic conditions and weather-related issues in Jakarta, as well as internal process inefficiencies.

secondary_ctqs: Blocked Orders Due to Overdue A/R

Man: Training for accounts receivable team on collection strategies
Staffing levels in the finance department
Method: Credit policies and terms for PT Smart
Material: Quality of customer credit evaluations
Documentation for credit terms and agreements
Measurement: Regular monitoring of A/R overdue percentages
Tracking of DSO and blocked orders

in_scope: Training and development for finance team
DN and GI process by logistics team
out_of_scope: Non-financial customer service issues
Supplier management outside of A/R impact

inputs.problem_text:
A/R is part of cashflow performance. In 2016,  36.20% of total A/R overdue in ID03 was trigger by PT Smart, so we will focus on this customer for this project.
High A/R overdue shows that a company is selling its product to customer on credit and taking longer to collect money. It means company has less readily available cash to finance our operation. In addition, high A/R overdue will be blocked the orders and CSD cannot send the product until the payment has been made by PT Smart.
inputs.goal_text: Reduce  monthly overdue A/R PT Smart to 12.8 %  by end of Nov 2017.

outputs.problem_statement: A/R is part of cashflow performance. In 2016, 36.20% of total A/R overdue in ID03 was triggered by PT Smart, so we will focus on this customer for this project. High A/R overdue shows that a company is selling its product to customers on credit and taking longer to collect money. It means the company has less readily available cash to finance operations. In addition, high A/R overdue will block orders, and CSD cannot send the product until the payment has been made by PT Smart.
outputs.goal_statement: Reduce monthly overdue A/R for PT Smart to 11% by the end of Nov 2017.
outputs.business_case: Reducing the overdue A/R percentage for PT Smart will improve cash flow, reduce blocked orders, and enhance operational efficiency, ultimately reducing costs and improving financial performance.

in_scope: Training and development for the finance team
DN and GI process by the logistics team
out_of_scope: Non-financial customer service issues
Supplier management outside of A/R impact

ctq_list.name: A/R Overdue Percentage
ctq_list.description: The percentage of total accounts receivable that is overdue for PT Smart.

ctq_list.name: Days Sales Outstanding (DSO)
ctq_list.description: The average number of days it takes PT Smart to pay its invoices.

ctq_list.name: Blocked Orders Due to Overdue A/R
ctq_list.description: The number of orders blocked because PT Smart has overdue accounts receivable.

Man: Training for accounts receivable team on collection strategies
Staffing levels in the finance department
Method: Credit policies and terms for PT Smart
Material: Quality of customer credit evaluations
Documentation for credit terms and agreements
Measurement: Regular monitoring of A/R overdue percentages
Tracking of DSO and blocked orders

measures: Monitor A/R overdue percentage monthly
Track DSO and blocked orders bi-weekly
Review credit policies quarterly

episodes.insight_md:
- The primary CTQ, "A/R Overdue Percentage," is selected due to its direct impact on cash flow and operational efficiency, aligning with the project's goal to reduce overdue A/R for PT Smart.
- The scope is focused on accounts receivable processes and credit policy adjustments, ensuring a targeted approach to address the high A/R overdue issue.
- Key risks include potential resistance to process changes and economic conditions affecting customer payments; assumptions include the availability of accurate A/R data and effective training for the finance team.
- Past successful audits in similar contexts highlight the importance of robust credit evaluation and consistent monitoring of overdue percentages.
- Leverage automated reminders and management software to streamline the collection process, as evidenced by previous projects.
- Next steps for Measure: 
  1. Collect and analyze baseline data on A/R overdue percentages and DSO for PT Smart.
  2. Develop a detailed data collection plan ensuring accuracy and consistency in tracking overdue payments and blocked orders.
  3. Engage with the finance team to validate data sources and ensure alignment on measurement criteria.

### Scope
- In Scope: Line 1 production operations, Operator training programs, Maintenance and calibration of production equipment, Quality control measures during production
- Out of Scope: Other production lines, Non-production related issues, Supplier management outside of raw material quality

### CTQs

inputs.problem_text:
A/R is part of cashflow performance. In 2016,  36.20% of total A/R overdue in ID03 was trigger by PT Smart, so we will focus on this customer for this project.
High A/R overdue shows that a company is selling its product to customer on credit and taking longer to collect money. It means company has less readily available cash to finance our operation. In addition, high A/R overdue will be blocked the orders and CSD cannot send the product until the payment has been made by PT Smart.
inputs.goal_text: Reduce  monthly overdue A/R PT Smart to 12.8 %  by end of Nov 2017.

similar_memory_episodes.insight_md:
- **Justification for CTQ/Y/Scope:**
  - The primary CTQ, "A/R Overdue Percentage," directly impacts cash flow and operational efficiency, making it crucial for project focus.
  - Focusing on PT Smart, which accounts for 36.20% of overdue A/R, aligns with the goal to improve cash flow performance significantly.
  - The project scope includes critical areas like accounts receivable processes and credit policy reviews, ensuring comprehensive coverage of factors affecting A/R overdue.

- **Key Risks/Assumptions:**
  - Assumes that changes in credit policies and collection strategies will significantly reduce overdue A/R.
  - Economic conditions could impact customer payment behavior, posing a risk to achieving the desired A/R overdue reduction.
  - Assumes that the finance team will effectively implement new training and strategies to improve collection rates.

- **Learning from Similar Past Episodes/Audit:**
  - Previous projects highlighted the importance of targeted training for the finance team in reducing A/R overdue percentages.
  - Past audits indicated that automated reminders and improved credit evaluations can significantly impact overdue reduction.

- **Actionable Next Steps for Measure:**
  - Collect and analyze historical data on A/R overdue percentages and DSO for PT Smart to establish a baseline.
  - Implement tracking mechanisms for blocked orders due to overdue A/R to identify patterns and root causes.
  - Engage with the finance team to review and refine data collection methods, ensuring accuracy and consistency in measurement.

Increase throughput from 5 tons/hour to 7 tons/hour.

### Business Case
Achieving the target throughput will reduce the cost per ton of product, enhancing profitability and operational efficiency.

### Scope
- In Scope: Throughput line operations, Training programs for operators, Monitoring and maintenance of production equipment
- Out of Scope: Other production lines not related to throughput line 1, Non-production related issues, Supplier management outside of material quality

### CTQs
- Throughput Rate: The amount of product produced per hour on the throughput line, aiming for a target of 7 tons/hour.
- Cost per Ton: The total cost incurred to produce one ton of product, aiming to reduce costs through increased throughput.
- Installed Capacity Utilization: The percentage of the installed capacity that is being utilized for production, aiming for optimal utilization.

similar_memory_episodes.summary_md:
## DEFINE PHASE SUMMARY — Increase Throughput Line 1

### Problem Statement
Throughput line 1 was currently 5 ton/hour from January to August 2025, while the target is 7 ton/hour with an installed capacity of 10 ton/hour. This creates a higher cost per ton of product due to lower output.

### Goal Statement
Increase throughput from 5 ton/hour to 7 ton/hour.

### Business Case
Increasing throughput will lower the cost per ton of product, improving overall cost efficiency and profitability.

### Scope
- In Scope: Line 1 production operations, Operator training programs, Maintenance and calibration of production equipment, Quality control measures during production
- Out of Scope: Other production lines, Non-production related issues, Supplier management outside of raw material quality

### CTQs
- **Throughput Rate**(Tons per hour ton/h) — The rate at which the production line processes material, aiming for a target of 7 ton/hour.
- **Cost per Ton**(Cost currency/ton) — The total cost incurred to produce one ton of product, aiming to reduce this cost through increased throughput.
- **Installed Capacity Utilization**(Utilization Rate %) — The percentage of the installed capacity that is being used for production, aiming for optimal utilization.

similar_memory_episodes.summary_md:
## DEFINE PHASE SUMMARY — proj_003_ Late_delivery

### Problem Statement
It's been more than 3 times that shipments to customer AAJ in Jakarta are consistently late, averaging 5-6 days beyond the scheduled delivery date.

### Goal Statement
Achieve 100% on-time delivery with no late shipments.

### Business Case
Improving delivery timeliness will enhance customer satisfaction, reduce complaints, and potentially increase repeat business, leading to higher profitability.

### Scope
- In Scope: Supply chain operations, Logistics and delivery processes, Scheduling and tracking improvements, Training for staff involved in delivery, Production processes related to delivery schedules
- Out of Scope: Supplier management outside of delivery impact, Non-logistics related customer service issues

### CTQs
- **On-Time Delivery Rate**(Number of on-time deliveries %) — The percentage of shipments delivered to the customer on or before the scheduled delivery date.
- **Average Delay Duration**(Average delay in days days) — The average number of days that shipments are delayed beyond the scheduled delivery date.
- **Customer Satisfaction Score**(Customer satisfaction rating scale (1-10)) — A measure of customer satisfaction regarding the timeliness of delivery.
similar_memory_episodes.problem_text: Shipments to customer AAJ in Jakarta have been consistently late, averaging 5-6 days beyond the scheduled delivery date, occurring more than three times.
similar_memory_episodes.goal_statement: Achieve 100% on-time delivery with no late shipments by the end of Q2 2026.
similar_memory_episodes.business_case: Improving delivery timeliness will enhance customer satisfaction, reduce complaints, and potentially increase repeat business, leading to an estimated 15% increase in profitability.

in_scope: Training for staff involved in delivery
Production processes related to delivery schedules
out_of_scope: Supplier management outside of delivery impact, specifically contract negotiations and supplier selection
Non-logistics related customer service issues such as product quality complaints

ctq_list.name: On-Time Delivery Rate
ctq_list.description: The percentage of shipments delivered to the customer on or before the scheduled delivery date.

ctq_list.name: Average Delay Duration
ctq_list.description: The average number of days that shipments are delayed beyond the scheduled delivery date.

ctq_list.name: Customer Satisfaction Score
ctq_list.description: A measure of customer satisfaction regarding the timeliness of delivery.

measures: Monitor on-time delivery rates weekly with a target of 85% by the end of Q1 2026
Track average delay durations bi-weekly aiming to reduce to 3 days by Q2 2026
Conduct customer satisfaction surveys monthly with a goal to increase the score to 8 by Q2 2026

inputs.problem_text: From August 2018 - March 2019 there were 52% of cases of differences in incoming Granule 238 (BGZ) data between physical and SAP data. which results in the TECO production process being hampered and the waste in the planning process because it has to check the actual physical stock in the field every day.
inputs.goal_text: Decreasing percent of cases of differences in data on incoming Granule 238 (BGZ) between physical and SAP to 10% starting November 2019.

outputs.problem_statement: From August 2018 to March 2019, there were discrepancies in 52% of cases between physical and SAP data for incoming Granule 238 (BGZ). This issue hampers the TECO production process and causes inefficiencies in the planning process due to the need for daily physical stock checks.
outputs.goal_statement: Reduce the percentage of data discrepancies for incoming Granule 238 (BGZ) between physical and SAP records to 10% by November 2019.
outputs.business_case: Improving data accuracy between physical and SAP records will enhance the efficiency of the TECO production process, reduce waste in planning, and minimize the need for daily physical stock checks, leading to better resource allocation and cost savings.

in_scope: Improving data accuracy between physical and SAP records
Reducing waste in the planning process
out_of_scope: Supplier management outside of intercompany supplier coordination
Non-SAP related data management issues

ctq_list.name: Data Accuracy
ctq_list.description: Percentage of cases where incoming Granule 238 (BGZ) data matches between physical and SAP records.

ctq_list.name: TECO Process Efficiency
ctq_list.description: Time taken to complete the TECO production process without delays caused by data discrepancies.

ctq_list.name: Planning Process Waste
ctq_list.description: Frequency of additional checks required for actual physical stock due to data discrepancies.

Material: Granule 238 (BGZ) handling

measures: Monitor data accuracy weekly
Track TECO process efficiency bi-weekly
Record frequency of additional checks daily

episodes.insight_md:
- The primary CTQ, "Data Accuracy," is crucial as discrepancies in SAP data directly impact the TECO production process and planning efficiency, leading to significant operational delays.
- A 52% discrepancy rate in data accuracy highlights a critical need for improvement to achieve the goal of reducing discrepancies to 10% by November 2019.
- Key risks include potential resistance to process changes from stakeholders and technological limitations in the current SAP system configuration.
- Assumptions include the availability of accurate historical data for baseline measurement and the capability of existing systems to support process improvements.
- Previous audits in the manufacturing supply chain have shown that improving data accuracy can significantly enhance on-time delivery rates and reduce operational waste.
- Learning from past process improvement projects indicates that focusing on training and system configuration can lead to substantial improvements in data accuracy.
- Next steps for Measure: Conduct a detailed data collection plan to establish a baseline for current data accuracy levels.
- Implement a pilot study to test potential improvements in the good receipt and data entry processes.
- Engage with stakeholders to ensure alignment and gather insights on potential barriers to accurate data entry and process efficiency.

primary_ctq.name: Data Accuracy
primary_ctq.description: Percentage of cases where incoming Granule 238 (BGZ) data matches between physical and SAP records.
